name: Adams, Anna Grace, Xq.
motto: "My friends monopolize me so, They make me go where'er they please; They really interpret my life; It's well I have some enemies."
text: "My friends monopolize me so, They make me go where'er they please; They really interpret my life; It's well I have some enemies."

name: Ashley, Louise, Xq.
motto: "O, mirror of noble womanhood."
text: "O, mirror of noble womanhood."

name: Austin, Russell H.
motto: "He plows deep and straight with all his powers."
description: Takes long walks on Friday nights for exercise (?).
text:
"He plows deep and straight with all his powers."
Takes long walks on Friday nights for exercise (?).

name: Best, J. Boyd
description: "Best" man in school even if he was a joke (editor) last year. He has a quiet way of saying things.
text: "Best" man in school even if he was a joke (editor) last year. He has a quiet way of saying things.

name: Campbell, William Peyton
motto: "Fusser as well as farmer."
clubs: Assistant to Commandant and Senior Cadet Major '16-'17
description: He is trying to prove that city folks can make good farmers, too.
text:
"Fusser as well as farmer."
He is trying to prove that city folks can make good farmers, too.

name: Cheever, Ed H.
description: A poet, though not many know it. He ignores trouble and woe.
text: A poet, though not many know it. He ignores trouble and woe.

name: Cochran, Maurice W.
description: A great shark but at what? Plays football and various other things. A good singer and a man "ready for his opportunity."
text: A great shark but at what? Plays football and various other things. A good singer and a man "ready for his opportunity."

name: Curnutt, Hugh A.
description: He prefers to be good rather than seem so. One who will let "the good fellowship of college broaden into after life."
text: He prefers to be good rather than seem so. One who will let "the good fellowship of college broaden into after life."

name: Daniels, Fannie
description: Great feelings hath she of her own, Which lesser souls may never know.
text: Great feelings hath she of her own, Which lesser souls may never know.

name: Ford, Clarence B.
motto: "I exist as I am, that is enough."
description: A politician pure and simple. Eligible for Presidency of "Sphinx."
text:
"I exist as I am, that is enough."
A politician pure and simple. Eligible for Presidency of "Sphinx."

name: Gilmore, Lucille
description: She does not agree that the phrase "Silence is golden," is trite.
text: She does not agree that the phrase "Silence is golden," is trite.

name: Gorg, Raymond A.
motto: "Here's manliness of manhood, a man that has a will for doing what he does."
description: A new man but respected by all.
text:
"Here's manliness of manhood, a man that has a will for doing what he does."
A new man but respected by all.

name: Greenhaw, Mary German
motto: "Karl's brother and therefore brilliant and well versed in law (and other things)."
text: "Karl's brother and therefore brilliant and well versed in law (and other things)."

name: Henson, John H.
description: He believes that perseverance is a means of gaining knowledge, so is with us again. Entered at mid-term and began his work. Always wears a smile.
text: He believes that perseverance is a means of gaining knowledge, so is with us again. Entered at mid-term and began his work. Always wears a smile.

name: Hurst, Floy
motto: "If at first you don't succeed, Don't let failure in your heart, Stand back and look things over once And take another running start."
text: "If at first you don't succeed, Don't let failure in your heart, Stand back and look things over once And take another running start."

name: Huston, Mary May
description: For real information see the representative of the "Alberts Teaching Agency."
text: For real information see the representative of the "Alberts Teaching Agency."

name: Jelks, C. C.
description: Peculiar indeed but perfectly harmless. A football demon and one who thought he should have had more honors while in the University.
text: Peculiar indeed but perfectly harmless. A football demon and one who thought he should have had more honors while in the University.

name: Jones, D. Webster
motto: "Good things come in small packages."
description: And this is no exception.
text:
"Good things come in small packages."
And this is no exception.

name: Johnson, Byron E.
description: Believes in many years of school work for "a well-turned furrow is one test of a good farmer; a well-trained mind is another."
text: Believes in many years of school work for "a well-turned furrow is one test of a good farmer; a well-trained mind is another."

name: Lawson, Hugh M.
motto: "Nuff said."
text: "Nuff said."

name: Lee, Arthur F.
description: A friend of the ladies but almost a bachelor this year. Some day he may reach his zenith (Zena).
text: A friend of the ladies but almost a bachelor this year. Some day he may reach his zenith (Zena).

name: Mather, Juliette Edla
motto: "Strictly Business" is her motto.
description: Miss Vaulx couldn't get along without her.
text:
"Strictly Business" is her motto.
Miss Vaulx couldn't get along without her.

name: McGaughy, John B.
motto: "What's the use of hurrying when there's plenty of time."
clubs: 1st Lieut. Co. B., Junior Class Football
text: "What's the use of hurrying when there's plenty of time."

name: McKnight, David A.
motto: "He is often the wisest man who seems not wise at all."
description: A good student and a hard worker.
text:
"He is often the wisest man who seems not wise at all."
A good student and a hard worker.

name: Miller, Paul Harwood
description: Another good judge. Quiet and unobtrusive but a brilliant fellow.
text: Another good judge. Quiet and unobtrusive but a brilliant fellow.

name: Mitchell, Emmett E.
motto: "Hath not the potter power o'er the clay?"
description: The man who discovers gold and other such minerals in the earth.
text:
"Hath not the potter power o'er the clay?"
The man who discovers gold and other such minerals in the earth.

name: Oates, F. Bonner
description: "A careful student of the humble bug," and once studied a bird for consolation.
text: "A careful student of the humble bug," and once studied a bird for consolation.

name: Quaile, Beatrice
description: As full of tricks as her name implies, As full of wisdom as she is replies. After all, what matters the size, If quality's there for us to surmise?
text: As full of tricks as her name implies, As full of wisdom as she is replies. After all, what matters the size, If quality's there for us to surmise?

name: Sailor, Vance L.
motto: "A man versed in the gentler arts, must needs next study law."
description: Learned to dance about three weeks before the Tri Delta dance. Wonder why?
text:
"A man versed in the gentler arts, must needs next study law."
Learned to dance about three weeks before the Tri Delta dance. Wonder why?

name: Scarlet, Winton C.
description: "Why?" his familiar expression. No, he's not the author of the "Scarlet Runner" but just a civil engineer.
text: "Why?" his familiar expression. No, he's not the author of the "Scarlet Runner" but just a civil engineer.

name: Sharp, James Edwin
motto: "Right noble in his merit for he has done the work of a man."
description: He is quite strong with the ladies.
text:
"Right noble in his merit for he has done the work of a man."
He is quite strong with the ladies.

name: Simms, Beatrice
motto: "Teach me half the gladness That thy brain must know."
text: "Teach me half the gladness That thy brain must know."

name: Smith, Ruth Cleveland
motto: "Modest, yet with all an Elf."
text: "Modest, yet with all an Elf."

name: Stevenson, James E.
motto: "Tall in stature, pleasant in mien."
clubs: Student Member Athletic Board of Control
description: The man who rode the steam-roller in '15-'16.
text:
"Tall in stature, pleasant in mien."
The man who rode the steam-roller in '15-'16.

name: Trimble, James W.
motto: "The reward of a thing well done is to have done it."
clubs: Student Secretary Y. M. C. A.
description: A man who has done more work possibly than any other in school. Finished at mid-term.
text:
"The reward of a thing well done is to have done it."
A man who has done more work possibly than any other in school. Finished at mid-term.

name: Walkup, Robert M.
description: A good fellow and a brilliant student. He never allows the girls to disturb his mind. "Bob" enjoys life and "labors on" for its reward.
text: A good fellow and a brilliant student. He never allows the girls to disturb his mind. "Bob" enjoys life and "labors on" for its reward.

name: Weld, Dana P.
motto: "Mind and muscle, brain and brawn."
description: He is more honorable than his honors indicate. Will make his mark as a chemistry shark.
text:
"Mind and muscle, brain and brawn."
He is more honorable than his honors indicate. Will make his mark as a chemistry shark.

name: White, Eddie Sonora
motto: "Why beat around the bush?"
description: Quietness is her loudest feature.
text:
"Why beat around the bush?"
Quietness is her loudest feature.

name: Wood, James Roscoe
description: He had to come back this year—was El-(len)-ated to do so—and will ever "wear the rose of youth upon him."
text: He had to come back this year—was El-(len)-ated to do so—and will ever "wear the rose of youth upon him."

name: Woody, Susan
motto: "Teach us sprite or bird What sweet thoughts are thine."
text: "Teach us sprite or bird What sweet thoughts are thine."

name: Wooten, William R.
description: A great yell leader and a "man who could build a church by squinting at a piece of paper." Made a dandy St. Patrick.
text: A great yell leader and a "man who could build a church by squinting at a piece of paper." Made a dandy St. Patrick.

name: Zoll, Allen A.
motto: "Hi there."
description: Acts well his part and receives his share of class honors. A debater as well as a football player; also a lover of tricks (Trixie).
text:
"Hi there."
Acts well his part and receives his share of class honors. A debater as well as a football player; also a lover of tricks (Trixie).

name: Amis, James W.
motto: "His tongue is that of a woman."
text: "His tongue is that of a woman."

name: Bird, Milmo
description: Many a Freshman has he steered in the proper path.
text: Many a Freshman has he steered in the proper path.

name: Bishop, Mark
description: One of us who is sure to make a "Mark".
text: One of us who is sure to make a "Mark".

name: Burrow, Frederick Hiram
motto: "A diamond in the rough."
text: "A diamond in the rough."

name: Campbell, Martha
description: Love is blind; she burned out her eyes striking matches on the east porch.
text: Love is blind; she burned out her eyes striking matches on the east porch.

name: Cantrell, Geo. A.
description: He never had a cold; is too slow to catch one.
text: He never had a cold; is too slow to catch one.

name: Casey, Jno. E.
description: Entered the university in 1898 to keep out of the Spanish-American war.
text: Entered the university in 1898 to keep out of the Spanish-American war.

name: Cherry, Rufus
motto: "At one time I thought all nights were for study, but not so now."
text: "At one time I thought all nights were for study, but not so now."

name: Clardy, Kelley
motto: "I judge so. Nuff said."
text: "I judge so. Nuff said."

name: Clark, James A.
description: You can always tell when "Pat" is well made up by watching the dance floor.
text: You can always tell when "Pat" is well made up by watching the dance floor.

name: Cole, Nellie
description: Of fair complexion and ways that make the "skin" the more attractive.
text: Of fair complexion and ways that make the "skin" the more attractive.

name: Craigo, Gladys
motto: "Charm strikes the heart, but merit wins the soul."
text: "Charm strikes the heart, but merit wins the soul."

name: Davis, Jeff
description: Jeff has political aspirations and bids fair to be elected to something.
text: Jeff has political aspirations and bids fair to be elected to something.

name: Evans, A. O., Jr.
description: Is said to resemble the famous Robert D.
text: Is said to resemble the famous Robert D.

name: Faist, Herbert
description: "Dutch" has acquired much dignity since joining the ranks of the faculty.
text: "Dutch" has acquired much dignity since joining the ranks of the faculty.

name: Felton, Lula
motto: "Flattery is the food of fools, Yet now and then our woman of wit Will condescend to take a bit."
text: "Flattery is the food of fools, Yet now and then our woman of wit Will condescend to take a bit."

name: Fish, Roy J.
description: "Topwater" is always nibbling.
text: "Topwater" is always nibbling.

name: Gibson, Thomas A.
description: Became one of Prof. Waterman's pets by his brilliance in "Ec. 2."
text: Became one of Prof. Waterman's pets by his brilliance in "Ec. 2."

name: Gilbreath, Bernice
motto: "Ah, what may a girl within her hide, Tho' an angel on the outside."
text: "Ah, what may a girl within her hide, Tho' an angel on the outside."

name: Gold, Marjorie
motto: "Cloudy the day or stormy the night, The sky of her heart is always bright."
text: "Cloudy the day or stormy the night, The sky of her heart is always bright."

name: Goode, Fannie Belle
motto: "'Tis beauty that doth often make women proud. 'Tis Virtue that doth make them most admired."
text: "'Tis beauty that doth often make women proud. 'Tis Virtue that doth make them most admired."

name: Grabiel, Ruth
description: Her tongue may be tied in the middle, but we know that it is loose at both ends.
text: Her tongue may be tied in the middle, but we know that it is loose at both ends.

name: Greaves, Bernice
motto: "She is a beautiful creature, depending on this beauty for much of her authority over her friends."
text: "She is a beautiful creature, depending on this beauty for much of her authority over her friends."

name: Hamilton, Scott
description: His main interests lie in the department of military science and tactics.
text: His main interests lie in the department of military science and tactics.

name: Harris, Robert D.
motto: "Bob" is strong for brooks, especially "Hornibrooks."
text: "Bob" is strong for brooks, especially "Hornibrooks."

name: Haynie, O. R.
description: Haynie gets more letters than anybody in school.
text: Haynie gets more letters than anybody in school.

name: Hay, W. C.
description: Clifton's long suit is watches and work.
text: Clifton's long suit is watches and work.

name: Hemphill, Mary
motto: "Too young to love? Oh say not so! Too young? Too young? Oh no! No! No!"
text: "Too young to love? Oh say not so! Too young? Too young? Oh no! No! No!"

name: Higgs, J. W.
motto: "He is in the matron's office."
text: "He is in the matron's office."

name: Hollabaugh, Gladys
motto: "To see her is to love her, And love but her forever, For Nature made her what she is, And never made another."
text: "To see her is to love her, And love but her forever, For Nature made her what she is, And never made another."

name: Hon, Mildred
motto: "Cloudy the day or stormy the night, The sky of her heart is always bright."
description: Frailty, thy name is woman.
text:
"Cloudy the day or stormy the night, The sky of her heart is always bright."
Frailty, thy name is woman.

name: Howell, Ruth
motto: "A maiden never bold; of a spirit so still and quiet that her motion blushed at herself."
text: "A maiden never bold; of a spirit so still and quiet that her motion blushed at herself."

name: Illing, Leo M.
description: His steel stock has not materialized and he is waiting still for his ship to come home.
text: His steel stock has not materialized and he is waiting still for his ship to come home.

name: Jacobson, Waldo
motto: "Don't touch the bristling pompadour!"
description: "Jake" is very "touchous" about his hair and claims that he has a right to be as it shows the result of many hours of brushing.
text:
"Don't touch the bristling pompadour!"
"Jake" is very "touchous" about his hair and claims that he has a right to be as it shows the result of many hours of brushing.

name: Leach, Luther O.
motto: "Let me wind my watch then I'll have time."
text: "Let me wind my watch then I'll have time."

name: Leverett, Nona
motto: "Nobody loves a fat man," but not so with the "gentler sex."
text: "Nobody loves a fat man," but not so with the "gentler sex."

name: McCoy, Aileen
motto: "She was so charitable and so piteous She would weep, if she thought she saw a mouse."
text: "She was so charitable and so piteous She would weep, if she thought she saw a mouse."

name: McCARTNEY, Norman
motto: What little time Mike isn't on East street he's going in that direction.
text: What little time Mike isn't on East street he's going in that direction.

name: McDonald, Dorothy
motto: There is not a more capable girl in school. Dorothy is always ready to do her very best, and her best is far above the ordinary.
text: There is not a more capable girl in school. Dorothy is always ready to do her very best, and her best is far above the ordinary.

name: McIlroy, Mertye
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"
text: "Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"

name: Massey, Joe B.
motto: Never cuts a date.
text: Never cuts a date.

name: Meadows, Coy T.
motto: "Sot" is there in a pinch.
text: "Sot" is there in a pinch.

name: Milburn, John B.
description: Would perhaps be a star at book selling in Rogers.
text: Would perhaps be a star at book selling in Rogers.

name: Mitchell, Wm. Moore
description: Is fond of Ec., but likes Mary better.
text: Is fond of Ec., but likes Mary better.

name: Moody, J. C.
description: Says that he would like to be akin to John Smith.
text: Says that he would like to be akin to John Smith.

name: Moore, Verda
description: There is nothing ill can dwell in such a temple. If the ill spirit have so fair a house, good things will strive to dwell with it.
text: There is nothing ill can dwell in such a temple. If the ill spirit have so fair a house, good things will strive to dwell with it.

name: Nelson, Irene
motto: "Oh! That this too, too solid flesh would melt."
description: Irene is entirely too thin (?) now.
text:
"Oh! That this too, too solid flesh would melt."
Irene is entirely too thin (?) now.

name: Parker, Elmo L.
description: Head of the weather bureau in "Buck".
text: Head of the weather bureau in "Buck".

name: Pedin, Orchid
description: Quiet and gentle is this girl of fair complexion.
text: Quiet and gentle is this girl of fair complexion.

name: Pender, Roy
description: Roy is a good winner if he holds the cards.
text: Roy is a good winner if he holds the cards.

name: Porter, Paul L.
description: Paul is among the best of the best.
text: Paul is among the best of the best.

name: Rankin, Fay S.
description: Fay is destined to make a preacher.
text: Fay is destined to make a preacher.

name: Reed, Reuben
description: Sleeps on one hill—and stays on another.
text: Sleeps on one hill—and stays on another.

name: Reichart, Christopher
description: Says that he is going to take "Trig IV" next year.
text: Says that he is going to take "Trig IV" next year.

name: Robinson, H. H.
description: Is usually "pressed" for time, but he never fails to say "Hello!"
text: Is usually "pressed" for time, but he never fails to say "Hello!"

name: Shadrack, William
description: A jolly good fellow. Usually seen around the "Hall."
text: A jolly good fellow. Usually seen around the "Hall."

name: Smith, Matthew M., Jr.
motto: "TOOT! Toot! Here comes the boy from Dallas."
text: "TOOT! Toot! Here comes the boy from Dallas."

name: Stevenson, E. E.
description: Won his letter in Fort Smith and lost it in Pottsville.
text: Won his letter in Fort Smith and lost it in Pottsville.

name: Taylor, Charles E., Jr.
description: I love school so that it is my plan to go over here as long as I can.
text: I love school so that it is my plan to go over here as long as I can.

name: Torbett, Harry E.
description: Thrives on "bull."
text: Thrives on "bull."

name: Wilson, William
description: Is Pat's roommate and they are off together.
text: Is Pat's roommate and they are off together.

name: Winfrey, George W.
description: The silent one.
text: The silent one.

name: Witt, Gibson, Jr.
description: Visions of luck pursue the boy. A Hot Springs product.
text: Visions of luck pursue the boy. A Hot Springs product.

name: Alexander, Margaret
description: "Oh, I just know I'm going to make a 'measley' grade in English II."
text: "Oh, I just know I'm going to make a 'measley' grade in English II."

name: Allsopp, James Edward
description: A follower of Epicurus.
text: A follower of Epicurus.

name: Alter, Glenn
description: Too bad that such a promising fellow has to live in such an environment as Hill Hall offers!
text: Too bad that such a promising fellow has to live in such an environment as Hill Hall offers!

name: Bailey, Frances
description: Well posted in the art of slinging chairs.
text: Well posted in the art of slinging chairs.

name: Bartell, Edward D.
description: Tall and handsome. Possesses a rich deep voice.
text: Tall and handsome. Possesses a rich deep voice.

name: Baumgardner,
description: An agriculturalist, an engineer and a Sophomore.
text: An agriculturalist, an engineer and a Sophomore.

name: Beard, Samuel J.
description: "Romie"—"Romeo" to the girls.
text: "Romie"—"Romeo" to the girls.

name: Bird, Beverly Ann
description: Holds the money for the Sophomores.
text: Holds the money for the Sophomores.

name: Bird, Marie
description: Short but sweet.
text: Short but sweet.

name: Bloom, Clarice
description: Translating German 2 c—"Jiminy Chris—?!"
text: Translating German 2 c—"Jiminy Chris—?!"

name: Boone, William B.
description: An exceptional man, like other Boones in history.
text: An exceptional man, like other Boones in history.

name: Bossemeyer, C. O.
description: C. O. made the Glee Club a success; —but not in Fayetteville.
text: C. O. made the Glee Club a success; —but not in Fayetteville.

name: Bradley, James
description: A brilliant student, a fluent speaker and some president.
text: A brilliant student, a fluent speaker and some president.

name: Bradsher, T. A.
motto: "I'll have nothing to do with tomorrow —I'll enjoy today."
text: "I'll have nothing to do with tomorrow —I'll enjoy today."

name: Campbell, Charles M.
description: Hurrah! One of the two Arkansas men on the Southwestern team.
text: Hurrah! One of the two Arkansas men on the Southwestern team.

name: Cantrell, Wm. Martin
description: Dr. Miser's math shark.
text: Dr. Miser's math shark.

name: Clark, Glenn
description: Is strong for his home town—Malvern. Taking a pre-med course.
text: Is strong for his home town—Malvern. Taking a pre-med course.

name: Cochran, Paul B.
motto: "A handsome countenance is a treasure."
text: "A handsome countenance is a treasure."

name: Coffey, Elizabeth
motto: "She believes in living now rather than in the hereafter."
text: "She believes in living now rather than in the hereafter."

name: Coleman, James W.
motto: "Wears an 'A' and worthily. He's afraid to go to Memphis now."
text: "Wears an 'A' and worthily. He's afraid to go to Memphis now."

name: Conley, George Dewey
motto: "A lover of mechanics."
text: "A lover of mechanics."

name: Conner, Laura
motto: "Let's go to big town." (B. T. F.) Big Town Fiend.
text: "Let's go to big town." (B. T. F.) Big Town Fiend.

name: Chotard, R. C.
motto: "The French are a gay people, fond of dancing and light wines."
description: But Bob has deeper qualities.
text:
"The French are a gay people, fond of dancing and light wines."
But Bob has deeper qualities.

name: Danner, John
motto: "He wears a perpetual blush."
text: "He wears a perpetual blush."

name: Davidson, June
motto: "Dotes on movies and enjoys life."
text: "Dotes on movies and enjoys life."

name: Dibrell, Artilla
motto: "A maid who is quite contrary, Full of jokes and always merry."
text: "A maid who is quite contrary, Full of jokes and always merry."

name: Dorr, Clyde H.
motto: "Here, kind reader, is a man."
text: "Here, kind reader, is a man."

name: Dudney, W. Cross
motto: "My zenith was not reached on Oct. 20th, 1915."
description: Can be found most any time about 20 Hill Street.
text:
"My zenith was not reached on Oct. 20th, 1915."
Can be found most any time about 20 Hill Street.

name: Ellison, Fred
motto: "Faithful to Periclean, Y. M. C. A. and Agris."
text: "Faithful to Periclean, Y. M. C. A. and Agris."

name: Evatt, Blanche
motto: "She is a quiet maid—at times."
text: "She is a quiet maid—at times."

name: Faison, B. S.
motto: "Beau Brummel, our Criterion."
text: "Beau Brummel, our Criterion."

name: Fincher, W. E.
motto: "Had seen a year in Hendrix, but never a place like Buck Hall."
text: "Had seen a year in Hendrix, but never a place like Buck Hall."

name: Fisher, Doris
motto: "Her lively looks a sprightly mind discloses."
text: "Her lively looks a sprightly mind discloses."

name: Forrester, Charlie
motto: "Why should I blush to own I love?"
text: "Why should I blush to own I love?"

name: Freeman, Leslie
motto: "I ain't, nor don't pretend to be much posted on psychology."
text: "I ain't, nor don't pretend to be much posted on psychology."

name: Furr, Beatrice
description: A dreamer of many dreams.
text: A dreamer of many dreams.

name: Gage, Margaret
description: She has a great big soul—it is worth your while to know her.
text: She has a great big soul—it is worth your while to know her.

name: Gollaher, Gladys
description: A teacher to be. Loves to keep silent.
text: A teacher to be. Loves to keep silent.

name: Grayson, Wm. M.
motto: "I never felt the kiss of love, Nor maiden's hand in mine."
text: "I never felt the kiss of love, Nor maiden's hand in mine."

name: Guthrie, F. E.
description: Big enough to defend himself.
text: Big enough to defend himself.

name: Hale, Harvey S.
description: A very mischievous fellow. Steals apples and throws bread in the Mess Hall.
text: A very mischievous fellow. Steals apples and throws bread in the Mess Hall.

name: Hall, Willis
motto: "Father's pride and mother's joy—the light of the household."
text: "Father's pride and mother's joy—the light of the household."

name: Hall, F. Preston
description: "Pret" is a man of leisure.
text: "Pret" is a man of leisure.

name: Hall, Mabel
description: The like was never read of.
text: The like was never read of.

name: Hardin, Temple
description: Almost worshipped by lovers of the great college game.
text: Almost worshipped by lovers of the great college game.

name: Harding, Horace
description: "Hun" won his spurs playing in the Kid Band.
text: "Hun" won his spurs playing in the Kid Band.

name: Harper, Constance
motto: "Did her father or her mother ever love her more'n me, Er her sisters or her brothers prize her love more tenderly?"—Scoops Craig.
text: "Did her father or her mother ever love her more'n me, Er her sisters or her brothers prize her love more tenderly?"—Scoops Craig.

name: Harris, Carolyn
description: She thinks too little and talks too much.
text: She thinks too little and talks too much.

name: Harvey, Ruby
description: This girl is always very good, Though she'd cut up if she could.
text: This girl is always very good, Though she'd cut up if she could.

name: Hays, Brooks
description: He likes to draw pictures and make speeches.
text: He likes to draw pictures and make speeches.

name: Hays, Hazel
description: Let gentlemen my strong enforcement be.
text: Let gentlemen my strong enforcement be.

name: Heard, Roger
description: A dancing demon and a football star.
text: A dancing demon and a football star.

name: Herring, Gillis
description: But work is work and must be done, Yet, as I work I have my fun.
text: But work is work and must be done, Yet, as I work I have my fun.

name: Hilton, Amelia
description: Her parents moved to the "woolly" west, But for education—Arkansas is best.
text: Her parents moved to the "woolly" west, But for education—Arkansas is best.

name: Hinton, C. B.
description: Percy's little brother. His favorite color— White.
text: Percy's little brother. His favorite color— White.

name: Hodge, Bess
motto: "Well sh-u-t up."
text: "Well sh-u-t up."

name: Hoepner, Clifford
motto: "On agin, Flanigin, off-agin."
text: "On agin, Flanigin, off-agin."

name: Hoeltzel, Pauline
description: "Yeth, mam, I made only thix As, but then you know I take rolling 'extherthises' in the parlor every night."
text: "Yeth, mam, I made only thix As, but then you know I take rolling 'extherthises' in the parlor every night."

name: Hollett, B. O.
description: His power of argument invincible, His power of truth, divine.
text: His power of argument invincible, His power of truth, divine.

name: Keener, Edith
description: And "keener" lightnings quicken in her eyes.
text: And "keener" lightnings quicken in her eyes.

name: Kernodle, Mary
description: Love to man's being is a thing apart; 'Tis woman's sole existence.
text: Love to man's being is a thing apart; 'Tis woman's sole existence.

name: Lamberson, Mattie
description: Quiet and unassuming.
text: Quiet and unassuming.

name: Landrum, John B.
motto: "I choose to write the things I durst not speak."
description: Our only short story writer who can compete with O. Henry.
text:
"I choose to write the things I durst not speak."
Our only short story writer who can compete with O. Henry.

name: Lee, Cornelia
description: Last and slowest would hurry, but she's afraid she couldn't stop.
text: Last and slowest would hurry, but she's afraid she couldn't stop.

name: Lee, W. M.
description: He is fond of his Coffee.
text: He is fond of his Coffee.

name: Lennox, Pauline
description: A spirit of mischief hid by a flowering face.
text: A spirit of mischief hid by a flowering face.

name: Lewis, Madge
description: Oh! I am a happy Sailor's girl.
text: Oh! I am a happy Sailor's girl.

name: Lipscomb, John
description: His home town ought to love him. John went 200 miles to help Bigelow win a county seat fight.
text: His home town ought to love him. John went 200 miles to help Bigelow win a county seat fight.

name: Little, Jane
description: She will have plenty of time yet to make a fool before she dies.
text: She will have plenty of time yet to make a fool before she dies.

name: Little, J. J.
description: June and Nat Goodwin are in the same class. Both have made good.
text: June and Nat Goodwin are in the same class. Both have made good.

name: Lutterloh, Charles
description: Learned the rudiments at military school. Is known as "Son" to his Jonesboro friends.
text: Learned the rudiments at military school. Is known as "Son" to his Jonesboro friends.

name: Lyle, J. E. (James Esau)
description: And Esau was a cunning hunter. The only Agri who has majored in English.
text: And Esau was a cunning hunter. The only Agri who has majored in English.

name: Machin, Hughes
motto: "I love to work and play tennis."
text: "I love to work and play tennis."

name: Mason, T. A.
motto: "Looks well to the foundation."
text: "Looks well to the foundation."

name: Matthews, Earl B.
description: Is envied for his accomplishments.
text: Is envied for his accomplishments.

name: McCaleb, Maxy
motto: "Sometimes I get tired of study and then I like to loaf."
text: "Sometimes I get tired of study and then I like to loaf."

name: McGaughy, J. P.
description: Demosthenes hasn't a thing on Jimmie.
text: Demosthenes hasn't a thing on Jimmie.

name: McGill, J. T.
description: "Oh, Coach! he may be bigger than me but he's not a Razorback."
text: "Oh, Coach! he may be bigger than me but he's not a Razorback."

name: McLacklin, Roy
description: A draftsman of note.
text: A draftsman of note.

name: Mixon, A. H.
description: A very quiet sort of fellow.
text: A very quiet sort of fellow.

name: Moffett, J. A.
clubs: Blows a horn in the band
description: Alfred hopes to be a doctor some day. In the meantime he blows a horn in the band and majors in Biology.
text: Alfred hopes to be a doctor some day. In the meantime he blows a horn in the band and majors in Biology.

name: Morgan, Gladys
description: If you think nothing—just keep on. But don't say it—e'er you're gone.
text: If you think nothing—just keep on. But don't say it—e'er you're gone.

name: Monteith, Mabel
description: The stars in Math. 16 are not the only ones she studies.
text: The stars in Math. 16 are not the only ones she studies.

name: Morrow, F. H.
description: As a crafty engineer, Franklin is superior. Exists in Gray Hall.
text: As a crafty engineer, Franklin is superior. Exists in Gray Hall.

name: Mullins, Hugh
description: Young but gets there any way.
text: Young but gets there any way.

name: Neely, Virginia
description: If you want to arouse her feminine curiosity just ask her about Norman Smith.
text: If you want to arouse her feminine curiosity just ask her about Norman Smith.

name: Nesbitt, James
description: Jimmie tried to stay out this year and couldn't.
text: Jimmie tried to stay out this year and couldn't.

name: Nyegard, John W.
description: He is the only training school teacher who can make his pupils work at night.
text: He is the only training school teacher who can make his pupils work at night.

name: O'Bar, Blanche
description: My doctrine is to lay aside contention and be satisfied.
text: My doctrine is to lay aside contention and be satisfied.

name: Oldham, W. K.
motto: "And now abideth scholarship, studiousness, and college pep, but the greatest of these is college pep."
text: "And now abideth scholarship, studiousness, and college pep, but the greatest of these is college pep."

name: Payne, E. R.
description: "Boots" has to maintain the standard set by three older brothers at the U. of A.
text: "Boots" has to maintain the standard set by three older brothers at the U. of A.

name: Perdue, Gordon
motto: "The very pineapple of politeness."
text: "The very pineapple of politeness."

name: Pierce, L. D.
description: Lewis overcame measles and mumps together. Controls the Harrison delegation.
text: Lewis overcame measles and mumps together. Controls the Harrison delegation.

name: Pyeatt, George
description: The boy from the suburbs of Cane Hill.
text: The boy from the suburbs of Cane Hill.

name: Prather, Marion
description: Oh, I chatter, chatter as I go to join the brimming "Brooks."
text: Oh, I chatter, chatter as I go to join the brimming "Brooks."

name: Pratt, Evangeline
description: Some nymphs there are too conscious of their face for life predestined to the gnome's embrace.
text: Some nymphs there are too conscious of their face for life predestined to the gnome's embrace.

name: Ragsdale, J. G.
description: From his trim lips smooth elocution flows.
text: From his trim lips smooth elocution flows.

name: Ramsey, Gene
description: I was never deep in anything but love.
text: I was never deep in anything but love.

name: Reed, Courtney
description: I did my best to take the measles too.
text: I did my best to take the measles too.

name: Rice, A. W.
description: Came all the way from Minnesota to attend Arkansas University. Is trained in the art of wrestling.
text: Came all the way from Minnesota to attend Arkansas University. Is trained in the art of wrestling.

name: Richardson, Jno.
description: John never misses drill. He will be in West Point some day.
text: John never misses drill. He will be in West Point some day.

name: Ross, Una
description: A kind heart, always fond and true, always jolly, never blue.
text: A kind heart, always fond and true, always jolly, never blue.

name: Russell, Jean
description: All fat is smooth and I am fat.
text: All fat is smooth and I am fat.

name: Sanders, C. B.
motto: "In the spring a young man's fancy lightly turns to thoughts of love."
text: "In the spring a young man's fancy lightly turns to thoughts of love."

name: Searcey, R. L.
motto: "In youth is the time to train the mind."
text: "In youth is the time to train the mind."

name: Sibley, Velma
description: Marriage for a great woman is a fall from dignity and ideals amounting to a deposition.
text: Marriage for a great woman is a fall from dignity and ideals amounting to a deposition.

name: Shackleford, J. M.
description: Wins honors both on the field and in the classroom.
text: Wins honors both on the field and in the classroom.

name: Shumaker, C. A.
description: Little but all there—Lover of the light fantastic.
text: Little but all there—Lover of the light fantastic.

name: Simmons, Una
description: If love alas be pain, the pain I bear no thought can figure and no tongue compare.
text: If love alas be pain, the pain I bear no thought can figure and no tongue compare.

name: Simpson, Verdelle
motto: "Miss Peck said so."
text: "Miss Peck said so."

name: Skaggs, Lester
motto: "Neat, not gaudy."
description: A student of the substantial sort.
text:
"Neat, not gaudy."
A student of the substantial sort.

name: Smith, Isabella
motto: "I like not only to be loved, But to be told I am loved."
text: "I like not only to be loved, But to be told I am loved."

name: Smith, L. P.
motto: "Ruddy, rough and ready. Takes care of himself."
text: "Ruddy, rough and ready. Takes care of himself."

name: Smith, Velma
motto: "Her stature tall. I hate a dumpy woman."
text: "Her stature tall. I hate a dumpy woman."

name: Sparks, John
motto: "Was ruled ineligible but he couldn't resist the temptation so he bossed the Freshman team."
text: "Was ruled ineligible but he couldn't resist the temptation so he bossed the Freshman team."

name: Stewart, Olive
motto: "A genial disposition wins its owner many friends."
text: "A genial disposition wins its owner many friends."

name: Styron, Mary
motto: "Jimmie is the 'Bane' of my life."
text: "Jimmie is the 'Bane' of my life."

name: Suggs, A. R.
motto: "Too dignified? Not so. A man of experience."
text: "Too dignified? Not so. A man of experience."

name: Taylor, Beloit
motto: "Beloit did his part in the Sophomore-Freshman game. Wears a beautiful pompadour."
text: "Beloit did his part in the Sophomore-Freshman game. Wears a beautiful pompadour."

name: Taylor, Bess
motto: "She never told her love, But let concealment like a worm in the bud Feed on her damask cheek."
text: "She never told her love, But let concealment like a worm in the bud Feed on her damask cheek."

name: Townsend, H. W.
motto: "'On the top of that bald eminence, no capillary substance could vegetate.' A keen whistler and a German shark."
text: "'On the top of that bald eminence, no capillary substance could vegetate.' A keen whistler and a German shark."

name: Turner, W. O.
motto: "The adroit engineer from the suburbs of Argenta."
text: "The adroit engineer from the suburbs of Argenta."

name: Tyson, Lucile
motto: "You look wise; pray correct the error."
text: "You look wise; pray correct the error."

name: Vestal, Howell
motto: "Pretty eyes, pretty hair— Howell knows who owns them."
text: "Pretty eyes, pretty hair— Howell knows who owns them."

name: Vinson, Clyde
motto: "The scholar of the class."
text: "The scholar of the class."

name: Walker, Byrnes
motto: "Byrnes toots the horn—both literally and figuratively speaking."
text: "Byrnes toots the horn—both literally and figuratively speaking."

name: Warren, Gladys
motto: "Quiet but always talks when around 'Dick.'"
text: "Quiet but always talks when around 'Dick.'"

name: Watts, Elizabeth
motto: "Worth her weight in Irish potatoes."
text: "Worth her weight in Irish potatoes."

name: White, Emma
motto: "Some girls do and some girls don't."
text: "Some girls do and some girls don't."

name: Williams, Kate
motto: "She lived through a Freshman year of many terrors, but has no troubles now."
text: "She lived through a Freshman year of many terrors, but has no troubles now."

name: Wilson, Adele
motto: "When I am gone, wisdom will be no more."
text: "When I am gone, wisdom will be no more."

name: Young, Estelle
motto: "I like fun and I like jokes, 'Bout as well as most o' folks."
text: "I like fun and I like jokes, 'Bout as well as most o' folks."
